page: Microsoft Azure HB368-240rs_v5 is a high-performance compute instance belonging to the HBv5 server family. Built on the x86_64 architecture with dedicated Intel processors, this virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 240 dedicated vCPUs paired with 448.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of approximately 1.87 GB of RAM per core.

The server is equipped with 15851 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. It is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, enabling low-latency, high-throughput inter-node communication. The instance does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its high physical core count and low memory-to-core ratio, this instance offers high compute density, making it cost-efficient for parallel processing workloads that do not require massive memory footprints per core. The combination of RDMA capability and extensive local storage makes it suitable for high-performance computing (HPC) applications, large-scale simulations, clustered database workloads, and compute-heavy engineering tasks.

It is RDMA-capable, facilitating high-speed, low-latency networking across clustered environments, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile delivers high compute density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for parallel processing tasks. The server is optimized for high-performance computing (HPC) workloads, large-scale engineering simulations, and clustered database deployments.
